content: We can reframe the performance gap between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), random network distillation (RND), and standalone curious agents as a function of uncompressed episodic memory storage allocated to unexpected state prediction, rather than just differences in reward formulation. This leads to the testable hypothesis that pairing either ICM or RND with a 2024 SOTA scalable active episodic memory architecture will close the performance gap between modular curiosity pipelines and end-to-end curious agents on long-horizon sparse reward tasks.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
